QQQ.data_anomaly: QQQ price $713.13 is outside expected range ($250-$700). Verify data source — may be contract rollover, unit mismatch, or genuine historic move.

macro_summary: Friday's session saw a broad risk-off move as the Trump-Xi summit yielded no trade deal and US10Y spiked to 4.60% on fears the Fed is behind the curve. The S&P 500 fell 0.8% to 741.84 while the Nasdaq slid 0.9% to 713.13, pressured by a 2.6% drop in SOXX after TSM announced a surprise stake sale and chip tariffs remained unresolved. WTI closed at $101.16, down 1.2% from intraday highs near $104, but still elevated as fresh ship seizures kept the Hormuz risk premium alive.
commodities_note: WTI settled at $101.16 after spiking above $103 on renewed Strait of Hormuz disruption; the 5-day gain of 6.0% reflects persistent supply fears. Gold dropped 1.3% to $4,561, pressured by a stronger dollar (DXY +0.25% to 99.29) and rising real yields. QQQ data outlier: price $713.13 is flagged as outside typical range—verify the source as this may represent a contract rollover or genuine breakout.

singapore.summary: All three banks are hard-blocked by RSI above 65, with DBS showing the most excessive reading at 81. US10Y at 4.60% is well above the 4.20% threshold where NIM compression begins to ease; no relief is visible until the October MAS decision. OCBC continues to outperform on YoY profit growth, but the gap with DBS and UOB is narrowing as loan growth converges.
singapore.thesis_status: Structurally impaired — US10Y and MAS policy keep NIMs compressed until at least October.
singapore.key_development: US10Y spiked to 4.60%, extending the dual-channel NIM compression headwind for Singapore banks.

semiconductors.summary: SOXX fell 2.6% to 516.58 as the Trump-Xi summit yielded no trade deal and TSM announced a surprise stake sale. All 11 semis names are CAUTION — sector beta is the dominant lever here, not stock selection. NVDA's May 20 earnings are the next sector-wide binary; a beat could reset extension, but the bar is high after a 27.3% one-month SOXX gain.
semiconductors.thesis_status: Structurally bullish, tactically extended — earnings season is the next gate.
semiconductors.key_development: TSM stake sale and no US-China chip deal triggered a broad semis selloff; SOXX -13.45 points.

big_tech.summary: MSFT surged 4.2% on Ackman's stake disclosure, the only BigTech name trading near prior support. AMZN and GOOG remain extended and hard-blocked; PLTR's defense AI premium is under pressure as R:R compressed. The cluster's median PE of 26.9x is elevated but not extreme — the real question is whether AI capex translates to revenue before the Fed tightens further.
big_tech.thesis_status: Mixed — MSFT's Azure AI momentum is offset by the rate-sensitive capex debate.
big_tech.key_development: MSFT's Ackman-driven surge restored near-term momentum, but the name is now extended.

interconnected.entry_note: Add to watchlist if LITE's momentum confirms AI optics demand sustainability; wait for a 10% pullback from current levels.

interconnected.entry_note: Add to watchlist if Cisco's blowout print (per Investing.com, May 14) is confirmed by Arista's next earnings guidance.

interconnected.name: Samsung Electronics
interconnected.reason: Labor strike threatens HBM supply; SK Hynix and Micron would benefit from any disruption — trade the duopoly.
interconnected.entry_note: Revisit after Samsung's labor talks on June 7; if strike proceeds, MU and SK Hynix supply tightens.

active_risks: Strait of Hormuz: Iran's Revolutionary Guards reported 30 vessels passing, still far short of pre-war 140/day; fresh ship seizures and a sunk livestock carrier keep supply fears elevated.
US-China trade: Trump-Xi summit yielded no deal on chip tariffs; US reportedly cleared Nvidia H200 sales, but no broader easing of export controls.
Fed policy hawkish pivot: Kevin Warsh confirmed as Fed chair; bond market signals rate hikes may be needed as April CPI hit 3.8% and PPI 6%.
Samsung labor strike: 18-day strike planned from May 21 by 50,000 workers; impact on memory supply uncertain, but could disrupt HBM production.
geopolitical.market_impact: Oil at $101 keeps inflation fears alive and rate-sensitive tech under pressure; semis sold off 2.6% as the trade deal disappointment hit NVDA and TSM. Singapore banks remain rangebound as US10Y at 4.60% prolongs NIM compression.
geopolitical.new_since_yesterday: Trump-Xi summit concluded with no trade deal; Trump hailed 'fantastic' deals but specifics were absent. TSM announced a surprise stake sale, sending shares down 2.4%.
geopolitical.portfolio_action: No new entries until VIX settles below 16.5 and the NVDA earnings binary clears on May 20; hold cash and wait for extension to reset across the watchlist.

portfolio_snapshot.risk_posture: Eighteen of 22 names are hard-blocked on technical extension; the entire watchlist is in wait-for-pullback mode ahead of NVDA's earnings on May 20.
